data.title: Hold Rating on Day One Biopharmaceuticals as Servier’s $2.5 Billion Takeout Caps Upside at $21.50 Target
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Tara Bancroft downgraded the rating on Day One Biopharmaceuticals to a Hold today, setting a price target of $21.50.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tara Bancroft has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors related to Servier’s proposed $2.5 billion acquisition of Day One Biopharmaceuticals. The offer price of $21.50 per share reflects a roughly 68% premium to the pre-announcement close and an 86% premium to the volume-weighted average price, which he views as appropriately capturing the value of Ojemda, DAY301, and Emi-Le.

Given that the agreed purchase price is in line with his discounted cash flow valuation, Bancroft sees limited additional upside for the stock from current levels. With the transaction expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, he believes the risk/reward profile is now balanced, prompting the downgrade from Buy to Hold and aligning the price target with the acquisition price at $21.50.

In another report released today, JonesTrading also assigned a Hold rating to the stock with a $21.50 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Sana Biotechnology: Advancing T1D and In Vivo CAR-T Programs with 2026 Catalysts and Solid Cash Runway Supporting Buy Rating
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Marc Frahm has maintained their bullish stance on SANA stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Marc Frahm has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, starting with meaningful progress on Sana’s lead T1D program SC451. The company has created a GMP-grade master cell bank for its HIP-edited iPSC-derived beta islet cells, and with toxicology work and manufacturing scale-up underway, management is targeting an IND around 2026, supported by prior clinical proof-of-concept for the HIP immune-evasion platform in a related beta cell therapy.

In addition, Frahm highlights the strategic value of SG293, an in vivo CD19 CAR T candidate that could substantially lower treatment complexity and costs relative to autologous approaches and is expected to deliver early clinical data in 2026, a timing that aligns with rising M&A interest in in vivo CAR assets. Combined with a cash balance of about $138 million, providing funding into late 2026, he sees Sana as well capitalized to reach these value-creating milestones, underpinning his positive view on the stock’s risk‑reward profile.

According to TipRanks, Frahm is a 5-star analyst with an average return of 18.9% and a 49.87% success rate. Frahm covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Incyte, Agios Pharma, and Kymera Therapeutics.

In another report released today, Bank of America Securities also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $7.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Advantage Energy: Undervalued Low-Cost Montney Producer with Sustainable Free Cash Flow and Upside Leverage
data.description:
Aaron Bilkoski, an analyst from TD Cowen, reiterated the Buy rating on Advantage Energy. The associated price target remains the same with C$14.00.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Aaron Bilkoski has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight Advantage Energy’s attractive risk‑reward profile and operational strength. He views the company’s low‑cost Montney asset base, efficient capital allocation, and disciplined balance sheet management as key supports for sustainable free cash flow generation over the medium term.

He also believes that ongoing productivity gains, incremental infrastructure optimization, and prudent hedging provide resilience to commodity price volatility while preserving upside leverage to improving gas and liquids prices. In his view, the current valuation does not fully reflect the company’s growth runway, improving returns, and capacity for future shareholder returns, justifying a Buy recommendation on the shares.

In another report released today, ATB Cormark Capital Markets also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a C$14.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
